title: Pulmonary Alveolar Proteinosis
chief_complaint: Progressive shortness of breath and dry cough for 1 year. I feel like I'm drowning in my own lungs. I've been treated for 'pneumonia' 3 times but antibiotics don't help.

hidden_data.cxr: Bilateral perihilar alveolar infiltrates (bat-wing pattern). No pleural effusion.
hidden_data.ct_chest: Crazy-paving pattern (ground-glass opacities with superimposed interlobular septal thickening). Geographic distribution.
hidden_data.pft: Restrictive pattern. FVC 55%. DLCO severely reduced 35%.
hidden_data.abg: pH 7.42, PaCO2 38, PaO2 55, SaO2 88%. A-a gradient elevated (45).
hidden_data.bal: Bronchoalveolar lavage: Milky, opaque fluid. PAS-positive lipoproteinaceous material.
hidden_data.gm_csf_ab: GM-CSF autoantibodies: POSITIVE (confirmatory for autoimmune PAP)

key_learning_points: PAP = accumulation of surfactant lipoprotein in alveoli due to impaired clearance by alveolar macrophages. Autoimmune (90%) from GM-CSF autoantibodies
Crazy-paving pattern on CT is classic (GG opacities + septal thickening). But NOT specific (also seen in ARDS, lipoid pneumonia, adenocarcinoma)
Diagnosis: BAL showing milky fluid + PAS-positive material. GM-CSF autoantibodies confirm autoimmune PAP. Lung biopsy if BAL non-diagnostic
Treatment: Whole lung lavage (one lung at a time under general anesthesia) - mechanical removal of proteinaceous material. Dramatic improvement after lavage
GM-CSF therapy (Sargramostim) for those who cannot undergo or fail lavage. Rituximab for refractory cases. Anti-GM-CSF antibodies track disease activity
